name: List Comprehensions
description: Questions on list comprehensions and readability.

questions.question: [x for x in range(5)] produces:
questions.explanation: List comprehension builds a list of each x in range(5).

questions.question: Which creates a generator (not a list)?
questions.explanation: Parentheses create a generator expression; brackets create a list.

questions.question: You can add a filter with:
choices: if at the end of the for clause
questions.explanation: Syntax is [x for x in iterable if condition] — if comes after the for clause.

questions.question: [[1,2],[3,4]] flattened with a comprehension:
choices: [x for row in lst for x in row]
[for x in row for row in lst]
questions.explanation: Nested loops in a comprehension: for row in lst, then for x in row, collect x.
